verify.feedback: All declared Tier-1 gates re-ran green on the integration tip (8c8fed6; rebase was a no-op) and the RBG red for REQ-8.2-fk was re-confirmed real by re-running the failing-first commit ced567e against a fresh database (1 failed). tsc --noEmit is clean and no smithers.db* files exist. However the reviewer's high-severity finding in review.json (approved=false) is CONFIRMED by live reproduction: triggerSummary() in src/pipeline.ts discards the `claimed` flag from claimRun() and always calls invoker.invokeExecutor(runId); executeRun()'s status==='posted' guard is not atomic, so two concurrent manual triggers for the same chat+window produced runCount=1 but outboxCount=2 (duplicate summary delivery). This violates the ticket's D2 claim contract ('claims a run row ... via INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING'): the durable claim must gate execution. Fix: only invoke the executor when claimed=true (or make executeRun's pending->running transition an atomic conditional UPDATE ... WHERE status='pending' RETURNING and no-op otherwise), plus an integration test with two concurrent triggerSummary calls asserting exactly one outbox row. Note: the worktree was delivered with the entire evidence bundle deleted (uncommitted deletions of plan.md, gates.json, all logs, verify.json); I restored from HEAD before verifying — the implementer must not leave the contractual artifacts deleted in the working tree.

findings.title: The loser of the run claim race still executes and double-sends the summary
findings.detail: The manual trigger path does not use the durable `(chat_id, window_start, window_end)` claim as an execution gate. `claimRun()` returns `claimed: false` on the losing side of `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`, but `triggerSummary()` ignores that flag and still invokes the executor; `executeRun()` only stops once `status='posted'`. On this tree, running two concurrent `triggerSummary()` calls for the same chat/window produced `runCount: 1` and `outboxCount: 2`, so one durable run row still yields duplicate delivery. That violates the ticket's D2 claim contract for the manual summarize path.

findings.title: The current worktree deletes the ticket's required evidence bundle
findings.detail: The review target currently has `plan.md`, `diff.patch`, `gates.json`, the required `test-output/*` logs, the required `rbg/*` logs, and the ticket decision docs deleted under `artifacts/smithering/build/smoke-fix-verify-1/walking-skeleton-summary-slice/`. O6 says absence of any required run-scoped evidence file is a gate failure, never a pass. I could inspect the committed copies via `HEAD`, but the delivered worktree itself is missing the contractual artifact set.

review.reasons: Not approved. I re-ran the named ticket gates (`pnpm test:e2e -t "walking skeleton"`, `pnpm test:integration -t "webhook dedup"`, `pnpm test:unit -t "window header"`, `pnpm test:integration -t "single store"`), plus `pnpm test:integration -t "schema conformance"` and `npx tsc --noEmit`; they are green, and the `assigned_run_id` foreign key now matches eng §4. The remaining blocker is behavioral: concurrent manual triggers for the same window still duplicate delivery because the losing claimant executes anyway. Separately, the current worktree has deleted the run-scoped evidence bundle that O6 requires for landing.
